NousResearch/hermes-agent.summary: Hermes Agent is an open-source AI assistant designed for persistent, cross-platform interaction and autonomous skill development. It utilizes a closed learning loop to curate long-term memory, generate new skills from experience, and build user models through dialectic interaction. The system integrates with 14 messaging platforms and supports six execution backends, including local environments, SSH, and serverless infrastructure like Modal. Users can swap between hundreds of LLM providers via a unified CLI without modifying underlying code.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement based on task experience
Multi-platform support including Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp
Six execution backends for local, cloud, or serverless deployment

NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling.summary: Hermes-Function-Calling provides a framework for the Hermes Pro LLM to execute function calls based on provided schemas. It utilizes a recursive loop to generate tool calls and execute them, with a default implementation using the yfinance library for financial data. The project supports both structured function calling and a dedicated JSON mode for generating validated objects. Users can extend the system by adding custom functions in functions.py or defining new Pydantic models for specific JSON schemas.
highlights: Supports function calling and validated JSON mode inference
Includes built-in financial tools via yfinance integration
Uses ChatML format for OpenAI endpoint compatibility

NousResearch/atropos.summary: Atropos is an environment microservice framework designed to facilitate asynchronous reinforcement learning for large language models. It utilizes a trajectory API that allows environments to send data and trainers to pull batches, decoupling the RL loop. The system supports a wide array of use cases, including code execution, multimodal tasks, and multi-turn tool calling. It has been used to significantly improve model performance in financial prediction and function calling benchmarks.
highlights: Supports dataset, online, RLAIF, and multimodal RL environments
Includes a trajectory API for scalable data collection and distribution
Enables training for complex multi-step interactions and code execution

NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution.summary: Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is a framework for the automatic improvement of Hermes Agent's skills, prompts, and code. It utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and propose targeted mutations based on failure analysis. The system operates via API calls without requiring GPU training, using a reflective search process to produce optimized variants. All evolved changes are subject to strict guardrails, including test suites and size limits, before undergoing human review via pull requests.
highlights: Optimizes skills, prompts, and code using DSPy and GEPA
API-driven evolution requiring no GPU training
Strict guardrails including full test suites and size limits

NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter.summary: The Hermes Paperclip Adapter is an integration layer that allows Hermes Agent to operate as a managed employee within Paperclip company systems. It functions by spawning the Hermes CLI in single-query mode and parsing raw stdout into structured transcript objects for the Paperclip UI. This enables the agent to handle assigned issues using its native tool suite while maintaining session persistence across heartbeats. The adapter also converts ASCII formatting to GFM markdown and supports multiple inference providers for flexible model deployment.
highlights: Supports 8 inference providers and 30+ native tools
Parses raw output into structured GFM markdown and tool cards
Syncs Hermes-native skills and manages session state persistence

NousResearch/autonovel.summary: autonovel is an autonomous pipeline that manages the end-to-end creation of full-length novels from a seed concept. It employs a modify-evaluate-keep/discard loop across five co-evolving layers, including world-building, character registries, and prose. The system integrates mechanical and LLM-based evaluation systems to refine manuscripts through adversarial editing and dual-persona reviews. The final output includes print-ready PDFs, ePubs, audiobooks, and landing pages.
highlights: Multi-stage pipeline from seed concept to print-ready PDF and ePub
Dual-persona review loop using Claude Opus for literary critique
Integrated audiobook generation via ElevenLabs and art via fal.ai

outsourc-e/hermes-workspace.summary: Hermes Workspace is a comprehensive command center designed to orchestrate AI agents through a unified web interface. It integrates with the vanilla hermes-agent to provide a zero-fork environment for managing chat, memory, and system operations. The platform features a Swarm Mode that utilizes tmux-backed workers for autonomous, role-based task dispatching and a Kanban board for tracking progress. Users can manage over 2,000 skills, browse MCP catalogs, and access a cross-platform PTY terminal from a single dashboard.
highlights: Swarm Mode with role-based dispatch and tmux-backed workers
Integrated memory browser, skills manager, and MCP catalog
Full workspace file browser with Monaco and PTY terminal

fathah/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native graphical interface for the Hermes Agent that replaces manual CLI management with a guided installation and configuration environment. The application uses the official Hermes install script to manage local or remote backends while providing a centralized dashboard for model providers and messaging gateways. It features a streaming chat UI with token tracking, a memory management system, and a persona editor for customizing agent behavior. Users can also configure scheduled tasks across sixteen different messaging platforms including Telegram, Discord, and Slack.
highlights: Guided installation and dependency resolution for local or remote backends
Supports 16 messaging gateways including Slack, WhatsApp, and Signal
Integrated memory system with support for Honcho and Mem0 providers

sanchomuzax/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI is a monitoring and configuration dashboard designed for the Hermes Agent. It operates as a non-intrusive layer that reads the agent's SQLite database and YAML files without requiring modifications to the agent's core code. Users can track real-time activity, manage scheduled cron jobs, and inspect session histories and skill sources through a responsive React interface. The system utilizes a FastAPI backend and a WebSocket-based polling bridge to provide live updates on system health and costs.
highlights: Real-time activity feed with cost tracking and model distribution
Read-only inspection of config files and agent skill sources
Full-text search for browsing and analyzing conversation histories

Euraika-Labs/pan-ui.summary: Pan is a self-hosted web dashboard and workspace designed specifically for the Hermes Agent runtime. It provides a centralized interface to manage agent interactions through a chat-first environment that supports SSE streaming and tool timelines. Users can discover and install skills from skills.sh, manage MCP integrations, and organize work into isolated profiles with unique memory and sessions. The system includes a setup wizard that automatically detects Hermes configurations and can run as a background daemon or system service.
highlights: Chat-first workspace with real-time SSE streaming and tool approval cards
Integrated marketplace for discovering and installing skills and MCP servers
Isolated profile management for separate sessions, memory, and API keys

mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills.summary: Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills is an open-source knowledge base designed to provide AI agents with the structured domain expertise of a senior security analyst. It utilizes the agentskills.io standard to deliver 754 production-grade skills across 26 security domains using YAML frontmatter and structured Markdown. Each skill is mapped across five industry frameworks, including MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0, to ensure unified cross-framework coverage. The library is compatible with over 26 AI platforms, including Claude Code, Cursor, and Hermes Agent.
highlights: 754 skills mapped to 5 industry security frameworks
Covers 26 security domains using agentskills.io open standard
Compatible with 26+ AI platforms and agentic workflows

conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing.summary: avoid-ai-writing is a portable writing skill designed to audit and remove detectable AI writing patterns from text. It utilizes a structured two-pass detection system and a tiered word replacement table to identify and fix "AI-isms" rather than relying on simple prompts. The tool supports multiple operational modes and voice profiles, making it compatible with Hermes, Claude Code, OpenClaw, and other agentskills.io-compatible agents.
highlights: Three modes: Rewrite, Detect, and Edit-in-place
Tiered replacement table with 109 words and 10 phrases
49 pattern categories for structural and fingerprint detection

wondelai/skills.summary: Wondel.ai Skills is a cross-platform library of specialized agent capabilities designed for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible environments. It provides pre-configured knowledge modules based on industry-standard frameworks like Jobs to Be Done, Clean Code, and various UX design methodologies. Users can install individual skills or themed collections via the Claude Code marketplace or a dedicated CLI tool to enhance agent reasoning in specific domains. These skills enable agents to apply established principles from software architecture, product strategy, and marketing directly within their workflows.
highlights: Provides domain-specific skills based on authoritative industry literature
Supports installation via Claude Code marketplace and skills.sh CLI
Covers software craftsmanship, systems architecture, and product innovation

DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills.summary: pydantic-ai-skills is a standardized framework for managing modular agent capabilities within the Pydantic AI ecosystem. It implements the Agent Skills open specification by providing a tool-calling mechanism that allows agents to discover and execute specialized scripts or instructions. The library uses a progressive disclosure approach, loading skill details only when relevant to minimize token consumption. Developers can define these skills through local filesystem directories or programmatically using Python decorators and dataclasses.
highlights: Implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI
Reduces token usage through progressive disclosure of skill metadata
Supports both filesystem-based and programmatic Python skill definitions

Agents365-ai/drawio-skill.summary: drawio-skill is a tool that converts natural-language descriptions and existing codebases into editable .drawio XML diagrams. It utilizes the draw.io desktop CLI to export these diagrams into PNG, SVG, PDF, or JPG formats. The project supports codebase visualization for Python, JS/TS, Go, and Rust, and includes a self-correction loop to fix layout issues like overlapping labels. It is compatible with the Agent Skills format and integrates with agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Copilot.
highlights: Converts natural language and codebases into .drawio XML diagrams
Supports ERD, UML, Sequence, Architecture, ML, and Flowchart presets
Includes 10,000+ official shapes and 321 AI/LLM brand logos

smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills.summary: This project provides a collection of official Chainlink coding skills designed to enable AI agents to interact with oracle networks and smart contracts. It implements the Agent Skills specification to allow agents to discover and activate specific capabilities based on the task at hand. Supported skills cover a wide range of Chainlink services, including CCIP, Data Feeds, VRF, and Data Streams. These tools help developers build complex on-chain projects, such as tokenized funds with real-time verification and cross-chain settlement.
highlights: Complies with the Agent Skills specification for agent discovery
Supports CCIP, VRF, Data Feeds, and Data Streams integrations
Installable via the Vercel skills CLI for project or global use

tlehman/litprog-skill.summary: The litprog-skill is a Claude Code extension that implements Donald Knuth's literate programming paradigm by transforming codebases into human-readable narratives. It works by generating a .lit.md file that weaves together prose, Mermaid diagrams, and LaTeX math with the original source code. The tool includes a tangler to extract runnable files and a reverse-sync engine to ensure the documentation stays updated when source files are edited. This approach helps developers document architectural decisions and facilitates easier onboarding through structured, essay-like code explanations.
highlights: Generates .lit.md files combining prose, diagrams, and source code
Extracts runnable source files from documentation using a tangler
Automatically syncs source file edits back to the literate document

esaradev/icarus-plugin.summary: Icarus is a Hermes plugin that provides agents with shared memory and a pipeline for creating replacement models. It captures agent decisions and work into a markdown-based directory, allowing different agent profiles to access a common knowledge base. This captured data is then filtered by quality and exported as training pairs for fine-tuning cheaper models via Together AI. Users can evaluate these candidate models and switch to them once they meet specific performance thresholds.
highlights: Cross-instance shared memory via markdown files
Automated capture of high-value decisions and outcomes
Pipeline for fine-tuning and switching to replacement models

chigwell/skilldock.io.summary: SkillDock is a registry and management tool for reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It provides a Python SDK and CLI that dynamically load an OpenAPI specification to interact with the SkillDock API. Users can discover, install, and upload versioned skills, while the platform supports a commerce flow for buying and selling skills via TON and Stripe. The system ensures skill integrity through SHA256 verification and manages recursive dependency resolution during installation.
highlights: OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI for API interaction
Versioned skill installation with recursive dependency resolution
Commerce integration for skill sales via TON and Stripe

black-forest-labs/skills.summary: This project provides official image generation skills for FLUX models, offering standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns following the agentskills.io specification. It functions as a technical resource for developers to implement text-to-image and image-to-image workflows through structured JSON prompting, hex color specification, and multi-reference editing. The repository includes a model selection guide for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 variants and can be integrated directly into Claude Code as a plugin. Users can access documented API patterns for asynchronous polling, webhook verification, and rate limit management.
highlights: Standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 model families
API integration patterns for polling, webhooks, and error handling
Support for JSON structured prompting and precise hex color specification

Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory.summary: Skill Factory is a meta-skill for Hermes Agent that automates the creation of reusable skills by monitoring user workflows. It functions by tracking session patterns in the background and proposing structured skill definitions when it detects repeatable sequences. Users can generate both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds directly from these proposals via slash commands or natural language. This tool streamlines the expansion of the Hermes ecosystem by turning manual environment setups and development tasks into permanent, executable assets.
highlights: Automatically detects and proposes reusable skills from active workflows
Generates both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds
Provides slash commands to manage, list, and save detected patterns

ReinaMacCredy/maestro.summary: maestro is a local-first harness that provides a durable structure for coding agents to manage long-running work. It uses a Rust-based CLI to track features, tasks, and harness improvements as plain files within a `.maestro/` directory, eliminating the need for external daemons or cloud state. The system enforces a gated state machine where work must be backed by recorded proof and QA baselines before being marked as shipped. It integrates with agents like Claude Code and Codex via specialized skills and hooks to automate the lifecycle of development.
highlights: Gated lifecycles for features, tasks, and harness improvements
Local-first storage of work and proof in plain files
Integration skills for Claude Code and Codex agents

tiann/execplan-skill.summary: ExecPlan is an Agent Skill designed to help AI coding agents autonomously manage complex, long-running implementation tasks. It utilizes a structured execution plan that allows users to review and approve the approach before implementation starts. The system tracks progress in real-time and provides rollback procedures for safe recovery. It is compatible with tools such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Goose.
highlights: Autonomous execution for long-running tasks
Real-time tracking of progress and decisions
Built-in rollback procedures for safe recovery

cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill.summary: Agentic-MCP is an experimental client designed to reduce token waste in Model Context Protocol (MCP) usage. It implements a three-layer progressive disclosure pattern that loads server metadata, tool lists, and specific tool schemas only as needed. This approach utilizes a socket-based daemon architecture to maintain persistent connections and support hot-reloading of server configurations. It is primarily a concept validation prototype to test the efficiency of the AgentSkills.io pattern within the MCP domain.
highlights: Three-layer progressive loading to minimize AI token consumption
Socket-based daemon architecture for persistent MCP server connections
Supports hot-reloading of server configurations without daemon restarts

armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge.summary: Skill Forge is a tool that converts code repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into verified instruction files to prevent AI agents from hallucinating API calls. It extracts function signatures, types, and patterns, linking every instruction to a specific file and line number or documentation URL. The system supports version-pinning and provides lifecycle tooling for managing skills across different versions. It is designed for library maintainers and developers who need reproducible, auditable instructions for AI agents.
highlights: Traces instructions to specific file lines and pinned commits
Generates agentskills.io-compliant skills from code or documentation
Provides version-pinned storage to prevent instruction rot

amanning3390/hermeshub.summary: HermesHub is a curated skills registry for Hermes Agent that provides a secure way to browse, install, and share AI agent capabilities. It utilizes the agentskills.io open standard and an automated security scanner that checks for threats like prompt injection and data exfiltration. The platform includes a creator marketplace supporting x402 and Micropayment protocols for premium skills, as well as an agent-to-agent feedback system for trust scoring.
highlights: Automated security scanning with 65+ threat detection rules
Creator marketplace with crypto and Stripe-based payment protocols
Agent-to-agent feedback system for reliability and trust scores

PederHP/skillsdotnet.summary: SkillsDotNet is a C#/.NET implementation of the agentskills.io standard designed to integrate agent skills into the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It uses a skill:// URI convention to implement progressive disclosure, allowing clients to discover lightweight skill metadata before loading full documentation or supporting files. The library provides seamless integration with Microsoft.Extensions.AI and supports automatic discovery of skills from various agent directories like Claude, Cursor, and Copilot.
highlights: Implements progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files
Integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based skill loading
Supports automatic registration of common agent skill directories

mem0ai/mem0.summary: Mem0 is an intelligent memory layer that provides AI assistants and agents with the ability to remember user preferences and adapt over time. It utilizes a multi-signal retrieval system combining semantic, BM25 keyword, and entity matching to manage user, session, and agent states. The project offers flexible deployment options including a Python/npm library, a self-hosted server via Docker, and a managed cloud platform. It is designed for applications such as customer support chatbots, healthcare tracking, and autonomous systems.
highlights: Multi-level memory for user, session, and agent state
Hybrid retrieval using semantic, keyword, and entity matching
Support for self-hosted, cloud, and library-based deployments

vectorize-io/hindsight.summary: Hindsight is an agent memory system designed to enable AI agents to learn from experience rather than just recalling conversation history. It utilizes a retain, recall, and reflect workflow to manage long-term memory, offering an alternative to standard RAG and knowledge graph techniques. The system can be integrated via an LLM wrapper for automatic memory management or through dedicated SDKs for more granular control. It is suitable for autonomous AI employees that need to adapt behavior based on user feedback and complex task performance.
highlights: Retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent learning
LLM wrapper for two-line integration into existing agents
Support for multiple LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini

greyhaven-ai/autocontext.summary: autocontext is a recursive harness that enables agents to iteratively improve their performance on complex tasks. It operates by testing goals against real evaluations, retaining successful strategies while discarding failures to create a structured record of work. This process generates playbooks, datasets, and optional distilled local models that subsequent agent iterations inherit to ensure consistent improvement. The system integrates with various providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini, and supports MCP for client-side tool catalogs.
highlights: Recursive improvement loops using real-world evaluation and feedback
Generates persistent playbooks, artifacts, and structured execution traces
Broad provider support including Pi, Claude Code, and MCP

elkimek/honcho-self-hosted.summary: This project provides a configuration layer for self-hosting Plastic Labs' Honcho memory backend, enabling cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent without relying on third-party cloud storage. It deploys the full Honcho stack—including the API, PostgreSQL with pgvector, and Redis—on local hardware using Docker Compose. Users can route memory extraction and reasoning tasks through any OpenAI-compatible API or local inference servers like Ollama to maintain data sovereignty. This setup allows Hermes to build long-term user profiles and logical observations while keeping conversation data on the user's own infrastructure.
highlights: Enables private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent via self-hosting
Supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider or local inference servers
Includes automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis

yoloshii/ClawMem.summary: ClawMem is a local-first memory and context engine that provides AI agents with persistent, on-device knowledge. It utilizes a hybrid architecture combining multi-signal retrieval, intent classification, and multi-graph traversal to surface relevant context from markdown notes and session transcripts. The system integrates with Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes agents via a shared SQLite vault. It enables agents to capture decisions, generate session handoffs, and maintain a self-evolving memory layer without cloud dependencies.
highlights: Local-first architecture with no API keys or cloud dependencies
Hybrid retrieval using BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs
Integrates via MCP server, Claude Code hooks, and agent plugins

plur-ai/plur.summary: PLUR is a persistent, local-first memory layer designed to synchronize agent corrections, preferences, and conventions across different tools and sessions. It stores knowledge as plain YAML "engrams" on disk and uses a hybrid BM25 and embedding-based retrieval system to inject relevant context into agent prompts. The system employs an ACT-R activation model where facts strengthen with use and naturally decay over time to prevent context clutter. It integrates directly with MCP-compatible tools, Claude Code, and Hermes Agent to automate the capture and recall of project-specific knowledge.
highlights: Stores memory locally as open-format YAML engrams with zero API costs
Uses ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale ones
Provides MCP tools for automated learning and hybrid context injection

amanning3390/flowstate-qmd.summary: FlowState-QMD is a local-first memory layer designed to provide coding agents with immediate project context from markdown documentation. It utilizes a SQLite-based vector store and an anticipatory cache that prefetches relevant documents by monitoring active agent session logs. This approach allows agents to access design docs, ADRs, and changelogs before initiating a reactive search loop. The system integrates via an MCP server and supports local execution using GGUF models and node-llama-cpp.
highlights: Anticipatory cache prefetches context by watching agent session logs
Local-first storage using SQLite-vec and GGUF models via node-llama-cpp
MCP-native server with automated configuration for major coding agent clients

robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus.summary: hermes-web-search-plus is a plugin for the Hermes Agent that provides multi-provider web search and URL extraction capabilities. It utilizes a class-aware auto-routing system (Routing v2) to select the best available provider based on the user's configured API keys. The plugin ensures cost control by capping provider work in research mode and offers flexible configuration via a standalone setup wizard.
highlights: Routed search and extraction via multiple provider backends
Capability-based configuration with no global required key
Customizable routing priorities and provider-specific auto-allow gates

42-evey/hermes-plugins.summary: Evey's Hermes Plugins is a collection of 23 custom extensions designed to enhance the autonomy, observability, and cost-efficiency of the Hermes Agent framework. The suite implements specialized logic for multi-model decision debates, smart task routing with fallback chains, and structured telemetry logging. These tools enable agents to manage their own goals, perform self-correction loops, and consolidate long-term memory into vector storage. Additionally, the project includes a bidirectional bridge for Claude Code and budget enforcement via Langfuse integration.
highlights: Autonomous planning, reflection, and multi-model consensus debate tools
Smart model routing with sensitivity filters and four-model fallback chains
Automated memory consolidation into Qdrant vectors and identity evolution

FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin.summary: The Hermes Weather Plugin provides a suite of 13 meteorological tools for the Hermes Agent ecosystem, enabling real-time access to observations, forecasts, and high-fidelity model imagery. It utilizes a Python-first architecture that offloads heavy computational tasks, such as NEXRAD radar rendering and ECAPE calculations, to high-performance Rust backends. The plugin features an automated bootstrap process that compiles necessary Rust binaries on first use, ensuring a streamlined setup for complex weather analysis. Users can retrieve verified model soundings, severe weather outlooks from the SPC, and global data via Open-Meteo integration.
highlights: Thirteen tools for NWS forecasts, radar imagery, and severe weather alerts
Rust-backed meteorological calculations for ECAPE, soundings, and parcel profiles
Automated bootstrap system for compiling and caching required Rust binaries

42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin.summary: The Evey Bridge Plugin is a technical integration that connects Claude Code with the Evey hermes-agent stack. It functions by automatically checking for messages or tasks from the bridge and injecting them as context into every prompt submission. The plugin includes specialized commands for running a 'Mother Mode' improvement loop and monitoring system health, including wallet and cron status. It is designed specifically for users running the Evey stack at a designated local directory.
highlights: Injects Evey messages and tasks into Claude Code prompts
Automates Mother Mode improvement loops via the /evey-loop command
Monitors health, wallets, and crons with quick status checks

nativ3ai/hermes-payguard.summary: Hermes PayGuard is a security-focused plugin that enables Hermes Agents to handle USDC and x402 payments through a gated execution model. It separates payment preparation from execution by requiring an out-of-band human approval stamp for transfers exceeding user-defined policy limits. The system supports Circle developer-controlled wallets, cross-chain CCTP transfers, and automated x402 micropayments for paid HTTP fetches. This architecture ensures that while agents can stage financial intents, they cannot unilaterally move significant funds without explicit operator consent.
highlights: Enforces human-in-the-loop approval for high-value USDC transfers
Supports Circle CCTP for cross-chain USDC routing and execution
Automates x402 micropayments below configurable policy thresholds

raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare.summary: This plugin integrates Cloudflare's Browser Rendering API into the Hermes Agent ecosystem to enable web crawling and content extraction from JavaScript-heavy websites. It utilizes Cloudflare's headless browser infrastructure to perform tasks like CSS-selector scraping, PDF generation, and full-page screenshot capture. The tool also features AI-powered structured data extraction using Llama 3.3 70B via Workers AI. Users can automate complex web workflows including sitemap discovery and Markdown conversion directly through agent commands.
highlights: Crawls websites with depth controls and sitemap discovery support
Extracts structured JSON data using Workers AI and Llama 3.3
Converts fully rendered JavaScript-heavy pages into clean Markdown

builderz-labs/mission-control.summary: Mission Control is an open-source dashboard designed to centralize the management and orchestration of AI agent fleets. It utilizes a self-hosted, SQLite-backed architecture to provide real-time telemetry and task dispatching without requiring external dependencies like Redis or Postgres. The platform supports multi-agent workflows with built-in quality gates and integrates with frameworks such as CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen. Users can manage agent skills, monitor costs, and implement role-based access control from a single interface.
highlights: Self-hosted SQLite stack with zero external dependencies
Multi-gateway support for CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen
Built-in Aegis review system for task quality gates

swarmclawai/swarmclaw.summary: SwarmClaw is an open-source, self-hosted runtime and framework designed for orchestrating autonomous AI agent swarms. It utilizes a multi-agent architecture that supports durable memory, delegation, and scheduled tasks across more than 23 LLM providers. The platform enables users to manage complex workflows through an interactive dashboard featuring an organizational chart view and conversation-to-skill learning. It integrates with various tools including MCP, OpenClaw gateways, and local CLI backends like Ollama and Claude Code.
highlights: Supports 23+ LLM providers including Hermes, OpenAI, and Ollama.
Features durable agent memory and reviewed conversation-to-skill learning.
Orchestrates swarms with delegation, heartbeats, and automated scheduling.

howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness.summary: Hermes Agent Meta-Harness is a standalone outer-loop optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes agents on verifiable coding benchmarks. It treats the agent as an execution backend and searches for optimal harness configurations—the code governing context collection and retrieval—rather than modifying model weights. The system orchestrates benchmark evaluations, tracks performance frontiers, and performs structured candidate mutations to find superior harness variants. It currently supports TBLite and TB2 benchmarks through a research-safe workflow that emphasizes archive analysis and deterministic search.
highlights: Optimizes agent harness code through automated outer-loop search
Orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations via Hermes
Tracks performance frontiers with structured candidate mutation and comparison

runtimenoteslabs/gladiator.summary: Gladiator is an autonomous agent arena that pits two AI companies against each other to compete for GitHub stars on identical repositories. The system uses Paperclip for orchestration and Hermes Agent workers to execute tasks according to distinct corporate strategies: growth-focused Blitz versus quality-focused Craft. A real-time dashboard visualizes the competition through scoreboards, task boards, and audit trails. Upon completion, the project demonstrates cross-company skill transfer by merging the rival entities and their learned memories.
highlights: Orchestrates 9 autonomous agents across two competing AI companies
Uses Paperclip and Hermes Agent for task execution and learning
Real-time dashboard visualizes agent tasks, code, and scoreboard metrics

1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent.summary: Hermes is a multi-agent orchestration system for OpenCode AI that automates complex software development lifecycles. It utilizes a master orchestrator to route requests through specialized pipelines consisting of 17 distinct AI agents. The system ensures software reliability by enforcing mandatory quality gates and security audits before deployment. It supports diverse workflows including feature development, bug fixing, and performance optimization through structured context passing.
highlights: 17 specialized agents for research, planning, and implementation
Mandatory quality gates via reviewer and tester agents
Priority-based conflict resolution favoring security and quality

supermodeltools/bigiron.summary: Big Iron is an experimental AI-native software development system that automates the full development lifecycle through a graph-gated workflow. It integrates Hermes Agent with the Supermodel Code Graph API to replace traditional file-loading context with structural intelligence, including call graphs and dependency maps. The system executes an eight-phase process—from planning to autonomous health checks—using specific skills and quality gates to ensure architectural alignment. While now deprecated in favor of the Supermodel CLI, it serves as a prototype for autonomous, graph-grounded code generation and refactoring.
highlights: Executes an autonomous eight-phase SDLC from planning to health checks
Uses Supermodel MCP to provide Hermes with structural code graphs
Enforces architectural quality through automated graph-diff phase gates

Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill.summary: The hermes-acp-orchestrator is a skill designed to standardize multi-agent delegation within engineering workflows. It functions by routing specific goals to specialized agents including Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while maintaining strict context isolation. The system ensures operational stability through configurable safety controls such as execution timeouts and output character limits. This tool is primarily intended for complex tasks requiring distinct phases like implementation, risk analysis, and final synthesis.
highlights: Routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code.
Prevents parent context pollution through isolated delegation workflows.
Enforces safety via configurable timeouts and output character limits.

numtide/llm-agents.nix.summary: llm-agents.nix is a collection of Nix packages designed to simplify the deployment and management of AI coding agents and development tools. The project provides a centralized repository of Nix expressions that automatically update daily to ensure users have access to the latest versions of various agentic CLIs. It enables developers to run a wide range of tools, including Claude Code, Goose, and Gemini-CLI, directly through the Nix package manager without manual installation. This infrastructure supports both open-source and proprietary agents, offering a consistent environment for terminal-based AI engineering tasks.
highlights: Daily automated updates for all packaged AI coding agents
Provides Nix expressions for both source-based and binary distributions
Supports major agents including Claude Code, Goose, and Gemini-CLI

TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is a community implementation that runs the Hermes Agent within an NVIDIA OpenShell sandbox to prevent unauthorized system access. It utilizes kernel-level enforcement to block dangerous syscalls, filesystem writes, and network egress. The system allows users to maintain the agent's full capability stack while applying strict security policies that can be adjusted without restarting. It supports a wide range of integrations including MCP servers, IDEs, and various messaging platforms.
highlights: Hardware-level enforcement of network, filesystem, and process limits
Dynamic security policy presets for strict or permissive access
Integration with 40+ tools and a pre-built skills library

0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent.summary: nix-hermes-agent provides a Nix package and NixOS module for the declarative deployment of Hermes Agent. It allows users to manage the entire agent lifecycle—including configuration, workspace documents, and systemd services—through a single Nix configuration file. The module ensures reproducibility by rendering settings into a structured CLI configuration while keeping sensitive API keys secure via external environment files. It supports both Nix-managed declarative skills and native Hermes CLI interactive skills, providing a stable foundation for production-ready agent infrastructure.
highlights: Declarative management of agent config, documents, and systemd services
Secure secrets handling via systemd EnvironmentFiles and sops-nix integration
Hybrid skill support for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installs

rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent.summary: Portable Hermes Agent is a Windows-native desktop application that provides a user-friendly interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring Docker or administrative rights. It operates through a Tkinter-based GUI or CLI, utilizing an embedded Python environment to connect with local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The agent features a library of 100 tools for tasks including music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation. Users can also build custom automation pipelines through a built-in workflow engine and a dynamic tool maker for wrapping REST APIs.
highlights: Zero-install Windows agent with 100 built-in tools across 20 toolsets
Local model support via LM Studio and portable AI generation servers
Dynamic tool maker and workflow engine for custom automation pipelines

xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker.summary: This project provides a minimal Docker sandbox image designed to package and deploy Hermes Agent. It utilizes the official upstream installation script to set up the agent and includes mini-swe-agent within the container. The image supports multi-arch publishing and allows for persistent state management by mounting the agent's home directory. Users can easily build specific versions using build arguments to target different Hermes branches or tags.
highlights: Includes mini-swe-agent and official Hermes installation
Supports persistent state via mounted home directories
Allows building specific Hermes tags using build arguments

JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server.summary: This project provides a deployment framework for running Hermes Agent as a persistent, headless Linux service. It utilizes systemd to manage the Hermes Gateway, ensuring the agent remains active across reboots without requiring an open terminal session. The setup integrates with the Nous Portal for model access and leverages the native Hermes cron scheduler to automate recurring AI tasks. This approach offers a stable, self-hosted alternative to manual shell loops or third-party hosting for autonomous server operations.
highlights: Configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service
Enables autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs
Supports headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian servers

ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes.summary: This project provides a Docker Compose stack for deploying the Hermes agent with an integrated web terminal. It utilizes a pre-built image from the GitHub Container Registry to streamline the setup process. Users can manage the deployment as a Git Repository stack within Portainer and access the interface via port 7681.
highlights: Includes ttyd web terminal for remote access
Deployed as a Portainer Git Repository stack
Automated image builds via GitHub Actions

raulvidis/hermes-android.summary: hermes-android is a remote control bridge that allows AI agents to interact with Android devices. It uses a Kotlin-based bridge app that connects to a cloud server via WebSockets, enabling command execution through Android's AccessibilityService. This architecture allows for remote automation without requiring VPNs, USB connections, or port forwarding. The system includes 38 specialized tools for tasks ranging from screen reading and typing to managing SMS and phone calls, and it also supports Android Automotive OS head units.
highlights: 38 tools for screen interaction, app control, and device management
WebSocket-based connection bypassing NAT and port forwarding requirements
Compatible with standard Android devices and Android Automotive OS

teknium1/hermes-miniverse.summary: Hermes-miniverse is a bridge that enables Hermes Agents to inhabit and interact within Miniverse pixel worlds. It functions through a three-part architecture consisting of a standalone bridge daemon, a gateway hook for state broadcasting, and a specific skill that allows the agent to consciously navigate the environment. This integration allows agents to maintain a visual presence, update their status in real-time, and engage in bidirectional communication with other AI entities. Users can deploy multiple bridges simultaneously to populate a virtual world with specialized Hermes instances.
highlights: Connects Hermes Agents to Miniverse pixel-based virtual environments
Synchronizes live agent states including working, thinking, and idle
Enables bidirectional messaging between Hermes and other world agents

Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council.summary: Hermes Council is an MCP server that provides an adversarial judgment layer for Hermes Agent to stress-test plans and actions before execution. It utilizes a multi-perspective deliberation process involving personas like Advocate, Skeptic, and Arbiter to generate structured verdicts on code diffs, claims, and risky commands. This approach helps prevent overconfident agent failures by producing explicit signals such as blocking risks, required checks, and DPO preference pairs for reinforcement learning workflows.
highlights: Multi-persona adversarial deliberation using Advocate, Skeptic, and Oracle roles
Structured safety gating with allow, deny, and conditional verdicts
Generates DPO preference pairs and rewards for RL training workflows

marlandoj/zouroboros-swarm-executors.summary: Zouroboros Swarm Executors is a bridge system that integrates local CLI-based AI tools into the Zo Computer swarm orchestration ecosystem. It functions by using bash bridge scripts to wrap tools like Hermes, Claude Code, and Gemini, allowing them to receive tasks via standard input and return results via standard output. This approach enables local execution of complex tasks such as security audits and web research while maintaining a shared identity and memory system across all personas. The project includes a health-check system to validate environment configurations and a registry for adding custom CLI tools as swarm members.
highlights: Wraps CLI tools into AI personas with shared identity files
Provides bridge scripts for Hermes, Claude Code, Gemini, and Codex
Routes tasks locally instead of through remote API calls

gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle.summary: Hermes Blockchain Oracle is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides Hermes Agent with real-time access to the Solana blockchain. It functions as a bridge between natural language queries and on-chain data by exposing specialized tools for wallet, token, and transaction analysis. The server enables users to monitor network health, track whale movements, and manage NFT portfolios through conversational interactions. By integrating directly with Solana RPC nodes, it eliminates the need for manual block explorer searches.
highlights: Provides real-time Solana wallet balances and token portfolio tracking
Detects large-scale whale transfers with configurable movement thresholds
Retrieves NFT metadata, collection details, and floor price information

junhoyeo/tokscale.summary: Tokscale is a command-line interface and visualization tool designed to monitor token consumption and associated costs for various AI coding agents. It functions by parsing local data files and databases from multiple AI clients to aggregate usage metrics. Users can track their activity through a native Rust-based TUI or a web-based dashboard. The tool supports a wide range of integrations including Claude Code, Hermes Agent, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot CLI.
highlights: Native Rust TUI for high-performance terminal visualization
Tracks usage across numerous AI coding agents and CLIs
Provides a dashboard for monitoring token costs and consumption

joeynyc/hermes-skins.summary: Hermes Skins is a collection of visual themes for the Hermes CLI agent that allows users to customize the terminal user interface. It utilizes YAML configuration files to modify elements such as banner colors, ASCII art, spinner verbs, and branding text. These skins change only the visual presentation without affecting the agent's personality or behavior. Users can easily apply these themes via a session command or by updating their permanent configuration file.
highlights: Customizable colors, ASCII art banners, and spinner verbs
Supports partial configurations via inheritance from the default skin
Includes diverse themes ranging from retro DOS to cyberpunk aesthetics

AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC.summary: hermes-CCC is a port of the Hermes Agent framework specifically designed for the Claude Code native skill system. It integrates the Hermes brain architecture directly into the Claude Code execution context, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth authentication. The project implements 46 core skills across categories such as MLOps, software development, and research, including specialized tools for RL training and vector database management. This allows developers to utilize Hermes-style memory, personas, and task routing while remaining within a unified terminal environment.
highlights: Port of Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills to CCC
Runs natively in Claude Code without separate processes or OAuth
Includes MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and RL training

unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser.summary: Vessel is an open-source Chromium-based browser designed to provide autonomous agents with a persistent operating surface while allowing humans to supervise. It replaces headless automation with a visible UI where agents drive the browser and humans audit or intervene in real-time. The system utilizes MCP for control and maintains durable state through named sessions, pinned tabs, and annotated checkpoints. It is built for integration with agent harnesses like Hermes Agent and OpenClaw to support long-running web workflows.
highlights: MCP-based control for external agent harnesses
Human-in-the-loop supervision with runtime controls and approvals
Durable state with action undo and agent-meaningful bookmarks

Abruptive/Ankh.md.summary: Ankh.md is an open-source framework designed to transform a single Hermes Agent into multiple specialized, project-specific instances. It works by detecting a local `.agent/` directory and dynamically overriding the default `hermes` command with a scoped agent configuration. This allows developers to maintain unique instructions, skills, and memories for different codebases or workflows without cross-project interference.
highlights: Per-folder scoping with local configuration and skills
Seamlessly switches between default and scoped Hermes agents
Supports project-local memories, tools, and custom instructions

roli-lpci/lintlang.summary: lintlang is a static linter designed to identify language-level failures in AI agent configurations, system prompts, and tool descriptions. It uses regex and structural heuristics to detect common failure modes without requiring LLM calls. This approach ensures deterministic, fast, and cost-free quality gating within CI/CD pipelines. It supports JSON, YAML, plain text, and .prompt files to prevent issues like tool ambiguity and infinite loops before runtime.
highlights: Zero LLM calls for deterministic, fast, and free scanning
Seven structural detectors (H1-H7) for agent failure modes
CI/CD integration with customizable failure thresholds and JSON output

0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes.summary: openclaw-to-hermes is a migration utility designed to transition users from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automates the transfer of agent personas, workspace files, model configurations, and credentials between the two ecosystems. The tool addresses specific limitations found in earlier migration methods, such as incorrect authentication formatting and incomplete workspace detection. It provides a safe migration path through dry-run modes and automated backup creation.
highlights: Migrates agent personas, memories, skills, and workspace files
Maps complex OpenClaw model configurations to Hermes YAML format
Handles credential transfers from local and system-level environment files

42-evey/evey-setup.summary: Evey Setup is a deployment toolkit designed to bootstrap a complete autonomous AI agent environment based on the Hermes Agent framework. It uses a multi-phase shell script to orchestrate a containerized stack including model routing via LiteLLM, local GPU inference through Ollama, and vector memory with Qdrant. The installer provides three service tiers—Base, Services, and Full—allowing users to scale from a minimal agent to a production-ready system with monitoring and workflow automation. It integrates 29 community plugins and pre-configures free model routing to enable zero-cost operation.
highlights: Deploys Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant via Docker
Includes 29 community plugins for memory, social, and quality validation
Automates configuration for free model routing and local GPU inference

ucsandman/DashClaw.summary: DashClaw is a governance and decision infrastructure layer designed to secure AI agents interacting with real-world systems. It intercepts risky agent actions to evaluate them against declarative policies, routing high-risk tasks for human approval before execution. The platform records verifiable evidence for every decision, tracks terminal outcomes to prevent double-execution on retries, and provides cryptographic attribution via OIDC tokens. It integrates directly with various agent frameworks including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and LangChain through plugins, MCP servers, or SDKs.
highlights: Intercepts and evaluates risky agent actions against declarative security policies
Routes pending approvals to dashboards, CLI, or mobile messaging platforms
Provides eight lifecycle hooks for deep integration with Hermes Agent

Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander.summary: Hermes Incident Commander is an autonomous SRE agent designed to automate the detection, diagnosis, and remediation of production infrastructure failures. It utilizes the Hermes Agent framework to monitor system vitals, classify incident severity, and apply fixes across CPU, memory, disk, and service layers. Following resolution, the agent generates structured post-mortem reports and automatically creates new prevention skills to handle similar future incidents more efficiently. The system integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time alerting and uses cron scheduling for continuous health audits.
highlights: Automates incident triage, root cause analysis, and remediation actions.
Generates persistent prevention skills and post-incident reports automatically.
Integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time P0 alerts.

Christabel337/job-scout-agent.summary: Job Scout Agent is an automated job search tool designed to streamline the discovery and tracking of employment opportunities. The agent utilizes Hermes Agent capabilities to fetch live listings from job board APIs, score them against user-defined profiles, and perform company research. It organizes findings into structured local directories and maintains a CSV tracker for easy integration with Google Sheets. This system eliminates manual searching by providing a daily summary of relevant roles with direct application links.
highlights: Fetches live job listings using browser-based API integration
Scores roles against skills, salary, and location preferences
Maintains an automated CSV tracker for application management

rodmarkun/anihermes.summary: AniHermes is a natural language anime media server that automates the process of finding, downloading, and tracking anime. It leverages the Hermes Agent for tool orchestration, using LLM understanding to translate user requests into actions like scheduling CRON jobs or analyzing images. The system integrates with qbittorrent for downloads and Anilist for watchlist synchronization across Telegram, Discord, and CLI interfaces. Users can manage their libraries, identify anime from screenshots, and receive personalized recommendations based on persistent memory of their preferences.
highlights: Natural language control via Telegram, Discord, and CLI
Automated downloads and tracking using qbittorrent and Anilist
Vision-based anime identification and parallel multi-source searching

JackTheGit/hermes-ai-infrastructure-moni.summary: This project is a reference implementation for building autonomous, long-running monitoring systems using Hermes Agent. It utilizes cron-based workflows to ingest AI research from arXiv and track infrastructure repositories on GitHub. The system automatically generates structured markdown reports covering compute risk analysis, cost projections, and trend dashboards. It serves as a template for developers to deploy headless, scheduled agents that transform raw research data into actionable infrastructure intelligence.
highlights: Automated research ingestion via arXiv API and GitHub ecosystem tracking
Scheduled cron workflows for risk analysis and cost modeling
Generates terminal-style dashboards and structured markdown intelligence reports

bigph00t/hermescraft.summary: HermesCraft is an embodied AI framework that integrates Hermes agents into Minecraft as persistent, interactive players. It connects the Hermes agent stack to a Mineflayer bot body through a custom Minecraft CLI and HTTP API, allowing agents to perceive and interact with the game world. Agents can function as individual companions or as part of multi-agent civilizations with private messaging and social memory. The system emphasizes fair-play perception by restricting agent knowledge to line-of-sight visuals and directional audio rather than using privileged game data.
highlights: Embodied agents with persistent memory, identity, and tool-use capabilities
Fair-play perception system using line-of-sight filtering and spatial mapping
Multi-agent social features including public chat and private messaging

hxsteric/mercury.summary: Mercury is a blockchain cash flow analyzer and Hermes Agent skill designed to provide transparency into wallet activities across Ethereum, Base, and Solana. It utilizes parallel subagents to fetch transaction data, identify six specific fraud patterns, and generate AI-driven trading strategies based on wallet behavior. The project features a multi-layer WebGL and Canvas dashboard for real-time visualization of inflows, outflows, and risk metrics. It operates as a standalone tool or an integrated Hermes skill for automated multi-chain investigations.
highlights: Analyzes transaction flows across Ethereum, Base, and Solana chains
Detects six fraud patterns including mixer interactions and dust attacks
Visualizes data via interactive WebGL and Canvas-based dashboards

bryercowan/hermes-embodied.summary: Hermes Embodied is a robotics extension for Hermes Agent that enables autonomous fine-tuning of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models through natural language. The system orchestrates a self-improvement loop by collecting robot trajectories in simulation or hardware, provisioning cloud GPUs via Vast.ai, and retraining models when performance thresholds are met. It integrates with the LeRobot framework and MuJoCo simulation to automate the transition from data collection to model promotion. This allows users to manage complex robotics training pipelines without manual machine learning expertise.
highlights: Automates VLA fine-tuning loops using natural language commands
Provisions and manages Vast.ai cloud GPU instances autonomously
Supports SmolVLA and GR00T models for simulation or physical hardware

0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent.summary: This project is a curated directory of resources designed to expand the capabilities and workflows of the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It organizes community-contributed skills, tools, integrations, and documentation into categorized lists for easier discovery. Users can find everything from deployment utilities and messaging bridges to specialized domain applications and multi-agent orchestration tools. The repository also includes maturity tags to help developers distinguish between stable production tools and experimental proofs of concept.
highlights: Curated list of community skills and plugins
Categorized resources for deployment and integrations
Maturity tags for assessing tool stability

alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book.summary: The Hermes Agent Orange Book is a comprehensive practical guide for using the Hermes Agent open-source AI framework. It explains the framework's self-improving learning loop, three-layer memory system, and automatic skill evolution. The guide covers deployment, security models, and the use of a multi-agent Kanban platform across 21 sections. It is designed for developers and AI power users to transition from command-line tools to a desktop-based agent ecosystem.
highlights: Guides on self-improving learning loops and Curator engines
Details on three-layer memory and automatic Skill creation
Coverage of 64 tools, MCP, and 23 messaging platforms

OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide.summary: This project is a comprehensive optimization and deployment guide for the Hermes Agent, designed to streamline the transition from installation to production. It provides a structured 24-part tutorial accompanied by runnable artifacts, including configuration templates, systemd units, and a one-command VPS bootstrap script. The guide covers advanced orchestration of coding agents, MCP server integration, and cost-efficient model routing across multiple providers. Users can implement diverse deployment paths ranging from simple Telegram bots to hardened production environments with full observability via Langfuse.
highlights: One-command VPS bootstrap for Debian and Ubuntu deployments
13 installable skills and 5 opinionated configuration templates
Integration guides for 22+ platforms and multiple coding agents

metantonio/hermes-wsl-ubuntu.summary: This project provides a comprehensive setup guide and automation scripts for running Hermes Agent locally on Windows via WSL2 or natively on macOS. It integrates llama.cpp for model inference with the Qwen3.5 model family, utilizing GPU acceleration through CUDA or Metal. The guide covers the entire stack including the Camofox browser server for web-browsing tasks and the Hermes HUDUI for a graphical user interface. By following this documentation, users can deploy a private, local agentic environment capable of browser automation and LLM interaction.
highlights: Automated installation scripts for Ubuntu (WSL2) and macOS platforms
Configures llama.cpp with CUDA or Metal for GPU-accelerated inference
Integrates Camofox browser server for autonomous web-browsing capabilities

mudrii/hermes-agent-docs.summary: Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI agent designed to learn and improve its own capabilities through a closed learning loop. It utilizes a unified core that manages persistent memory in SQLite, autonomous skill creation, and a pluggable transport layer for diverse model providers. The system supports deployment across various environments, including a CLI with an Ink-based TUI and a multi-platform messaging gateway. It integrates with numerous LLM providers and external services like Spotify to execute complex, tool-based workflows.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement loop
Multi-platform gateway supporting Discord, Telegram, Slack, and more
Pluggable architecture for custom tools and model providers

martymcenroe/HermesWiki.summary: HermesWiki is a documentation hub designed to support the Hermes autonomous AI email agent. It provides a centralized knowledge base for users to find technical guidance. The wiki focuses on offering deployment patterns and specific recipes for implementing the agent.
highlights: Documentation for the Hermes AI email agent
Deployment patterns for system setup
Implementation recipes for agent configuration

nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel.summary: This project is a self-improving AI agent designed for autonomous execution with integrated safety boundaries. It utilizes a closed learning loop to create and refine skills from experience while employing a CaMeL-style runtime guard to prevent indirect prompt injections. The agent supports a wide array of LLM providers and can be deployed across various environments, including serverless infrastructure and cloud VMs. Users can interact with the agent via a dedicated terminal interface or through messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, and Slack.
highlights: Self-improving learning loop for autonomous skill creation and refinement
CaMeL-style runtime guard to block unauthorized sensitive tool side effects
Multi-platform access via CLI and various messaging gateway integrations

kaminocorp/hermes-alpha.summary: Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can bootstrap an autonomous bug bounty system from first principles. It utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent Overseer agent builds, deploys, and iteratively improves a tactical Hunter agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. The system operates via a FastAPI-powered web terminal and leverages Elephantasm for memory streams to refine the Hunter's skills based on real-world outcomes. This approach tests whether an agent can evolve its own codebase and strategy without pre-built scaffolding or custom tools.
highlights: Self-improving loop where Overseer evolves Hunter's code and strategy
Deployed on Fly.io with a FastAPI and xterm.js web terminal
Integrated with Elephantasm for long-term memory and performance monitoring

beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation.summary: This project provides a skill distillation pipeline designed to fine-tune Hermes 4 models using real-world agentic trajectories. It captures tool-use sessions from a diverse 30-task battery and converts them into SFT-ready data or live RL signals for the Atropos training framework. The system uses a multi-dimensional reward function to score trajectories based on task completion, efficiency, and error recovery. This approach enables a closed learning loop where production-style agent interactions serve as the primary training data for future model iterations.
highlights: Generates SFT-ready JSONL data from real-world tool usage trajectories
Integrates with Atropos for live reinforcement learning via GRPO
Automated scoring based on completion, efficiency, and error recovery

xaspx/hermes-control-interface.summary: Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to centralize the management of the Hermes AI agent stack. It utilizes a Node.js and Express backend with a Vanilla JS frontend to provide real-time control over agents, files, and system metrics. The interface includes a specialized swarm monitor for tracking agent health and task pipelines via a kanban system. It also integrates a full control plane for managing MCP servers and tracking token usage and costs.
highlights: ZOO Swarm Monitor with kanban pipeline and live event feed
Full MCP server control plane with config editor and log tailing
Real-time chat with streaming, multi-profile support, and tool call cards

ksimback/hermes-ecosystem.summary: Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub star counts and growth trends cached via Redis.
highlights: Directory of 80+ security-reviewed Hermes Agent tools and integrations
RAG chatbot providing grounded answers from 27 research files
Live GitHub star tracking with Redis-cached sparklines and trends

dodo-reach/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a dedicated workbench for interacting with Hermes Agent. It connects directly to the Hermes host via SSH to ensure the remote machine remains the sole source of truth without requiring a local mirror or gateway API. The app integrates sessions, Kanban boards, cron jobs, and file editing into a single window, including an embedded terminal for direct shell access. It supports multiple Hermes profiles for multi-agent workflows and is compatible with various hosts, including VPS, Raspberry Pi, and local machines.
highlights: Direct SSH connection without browser wrappers or daemon requirements
Integrated views for sessions, Kanban, skills, and cron jobs
Support for multiple Hermes profiles and universal macOS architecture

clawshell/clawshell.summary: ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provides secure email isolation through IMAP sender filtering and supports OAuth-based authentication for providers like OpenAI.
highlights: Maps virtual keys to real credentials stored in protected Unix directories
Scans and redacts PII in request and response bodies via regex
Enforces sender-based email filtering for secure mailbox access

nesquena/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI is a self-hosted web interface that provides a graphical frontend for the Hermes Agent. It is built using Python and vanilla JavaScript without the need for a build step or external frameworks. The application offers a three-panel layout for managing chat sessions, workspace files, and agent settings with full feature parity to the CLI. It integrates directly with existing Hermes Agent setups and models, allowing secure access via SSH tunnels.
highlights: Full feature parity with the Hermes CLI experience
Three-panel layout with workspace file browser and inline preview
Lightweight architecture using Python and vanilla JavaScript

garrytan/gbrain.summary: GBrain is a memory and context layer for AI agents that transforms raw page retrieval into synthesized answers. It combines a synthesis layer for cited prose with a self-wiring knowledge graph that extracts typed entity relationships without LLM calls. The system can be deployed as a personal knowledge base or a scoped company brain with multi-user access controls. It integrates with agents like OpenClaw and Hermes, as well as coding tools like Claude Code and Codex via MCP.
highlights: Synthesizes cited answers with explicit gap analysis of missing info
Self-wiring knowledge graph for complex entity relationship queries
Supports scoped multi-user access for institutional company memory

pyrate-llama/hermes-ui.summary: Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web interface that serves as a command center for the self-hosted Hermes Agent. It is built as a single-file HTML application using React 18 and communicates via a lightweight Python proxy server. The interface allows users to manage AI tasks, browse files, and monitor real-time logs and system health. It integrates specialized tools for managing MCP servers, cron jobs, and installed skills within a unified workspace.
highlights: Single-file React 18 HTML application with glassmorphic design
Integrated file browser, terminal, and real-time log streaming
Management tools for MCP servers, cron jobs, and AI skills

kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc.summary: This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and direct shell access with built-in safety filters for dangerous operations.
highlights: Starts Claude Code remote control sessions via Telegram slash commands
Provides mobile access to local filesystems and MCP servers
Executes headless commands and shell operations on the host machine

AaronWong1999/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is an integration bridge that allows Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and OpenCode to operate simultaneously on a single WeChat account. It acts as a central iLink poller and routes messages through local proxy servers to prevent the 403 errors and connection drops caused by multiple gateways competing for the same API token. This architecture enables users to switch between different AI brains or activate multiple agents at once using simple chat commands. It also introduces WeChat support for OpenCode, enabling voice-based coding tasks and access to free models like MiniMax M2.5.
highlights: Runs three AI agents simultaneously on one WeChat account
Prevents iLink token conflicts via local proxy routing
Enables agent switching with /hermes, /openclaw, and /opencode commands

stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm.summary: Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard flat summarization with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) engine backed by a SQLite message store. This architecture allows the agent to compact old context into depth-aware summary nodes while retaining the ability to drill back into raw messages using specialized tools. The system includes features for sensitive data redaction, large payload externalization, and source-aware retrieval to maintain high-quality recall without flooding the active context window.
highlights: Uses a hierarchical DAG to compact context without losing details
Provides agent tools for grepping and expanding summarized conversation history
Persists raw messages and summaries in a local SQLite database

Codename-11/hermes-relay.summary: Hermes-Relay is a remote-control ecosystem that allows users to interact with a server-deployed Hermes agent via mobile and desktop interfaces. It uses a WSS relay to connect a native Android app and a desktop CLI/tray application to a central agent. The Android app enables screen reading and device interaction, while the desktop client allows the agent to execute local system commands and file operations. This architecture enables a single agent to manage multiple device surfaces without requiring SSH.
highlights: Android app for screen reading, voice, and device control
Desktop CLI and tray app for remote server interaction
Bidirectional tool execution for local file and terminal access

clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp.summary: This project is a terminal-style web interface that allows users to interact with their Hermes agent directly within a Telegram Mini App. It implements a React SPA dashboard that connects to the Hermes Agent via a Python-based web server and a secure gateway. The interface provides a mobile-first TUI for managing agent instances, monitoring system health, and handling scheduled cron jobs. It features a dual-layer authentication system using HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 to ensure secure access.
highlights: Terminal chat with streaming responses and file attachments
Background agent spawning with live output monitoring
System health gauges and cron job management

briancaffey/hermes-otel.summary: hermes-otel is an OpenTelemetry plugin for Hermes Agent that provides observability for LLM-driven workflows. It automatically captures and exports model invocations, tool calls, and API requests as OTel spans to OTLP-compatible backends. The plugin supports both single-backend configuration via environment variables and multi-backend fan-out via a YAML configuration file. It integrates with a wide range of tracing and monitoring platforms including Phoenix, Langfuse, LangSmith, and Grafana Tempo.
highlights: Exports LLM tool calls and model invocations as OTel spans
Supports multi-backend fan-out via config.yaml
Compatible with Phoenix, Langfuse, SigNoz, and other OTLP backends

EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui.summary: Hermes Studio is a comprehensive management dashboard and local runtime environment for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It provides a centralized control plane for configuring multi-platform AI channels, managing local model providers, and orchestrating automated cron jobs. Users can interact with agents through a streaming chat interface that supports tool traces, file management, and persistent session storage. The platform also includes specialized tools for coding agents, group-chat rooms with context compression, and detailed usage analytics.
highlights: Unified configuration for Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, and Matrix channels
Local profile management with scoped memory, skills, and model providers
Integrated file browser and terminal for local and remote backends

Web3CZ/Web3Hermes.summary: Web3Hermes is a lightweight WebUI for Hermes Agent specifically localized for Chinese-speaking users. It provides a desktop-optimized interface that simplifies interaction with the agent through a localized workflow and full Chinese language support. The platform integrates a built-in workspace file browser, session management tools, and support for multiple AI models including OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek. Users can manage tasks, edit code with syntax highlighting, and visualize tool calls within a responsive dark or light themed environment.
highlights: Localized Chinese interface with dark and light theme support
Integrated workspace file browser for direct file editing and management
Comprehensive session management including export, search, and tool call visualization

AxDSan/mnemosyne.summary: Mnemosyne is a universal memory layer designed to provide AI agents with persistent, long-term context without requiring external cloud services. It utilizes a SQLite-backed architecture incorporating sqlite-vec and FTS5 for efficient storage and retrieval. The system supports various integration methods including a Python SDK and the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It is compatible with platforms such as Cursor, Claude Code, OpenWebUI, and the Hermes Agent.
highlights: Zero-dependency SQLite storage with sub-millisecond retrieval latency
MCP support for seamless integration with multiple agent frameworks
Advanced memory management including temporal triples and episodic compression

keepnotes-ai/keep.summary: Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that facilitates long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media—by summarizing, embedding, and indexing them into a searchable graph. The system uses 'edge tags' to create navigable relationships between notes, such as linking authors to their specific contributions. It integrates with various agent frameworks via MCP and provides structured prompts to help agents reflect on past actions and current intentions.
highlights: Semantic search with BM25 full-text and ranked graph traversal
Automated indexing for local directories, URLs, and multimedia files
Edge tags create navigable relationships with automatic inverse links

AMAP-ML/SkillClaw.summary: SkillClaw is a skill management framework that enables AI agents to evolve their capabilities through real-world interactions. It functions by running a background evolution loop that automatically deduplicates, improves, and merges skills across different sessions and devices. The system creates a unified skill library that synchronizes experience between multiple agents and users, ensuring that improvements made by one instance are available to all others. It natively integrates with the Hermes Agent ecosystem and supports various other platforms including OpenClaw, QwenPaw, and OpenAI-compatible APIs.
highlights: Automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops
Unifies skill libraries across multiple agents, devices, and user teams
Natively integrates with Hermes, OpenClaw, and OpenAI-compatible APIs

aivrar/portable-hermes-agent.summary: Portable Hermes Agent is a standalone Windows desktop application that provides a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring system-wide installations or administrative rights. It operates by bundling an embedded Python environment and connecting to local models via LM Studio or cloud providers through an OpenAI-compatible API.
