The platform features over 100 tools for tasks such as music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation, alongside a workflow engine for multi-step automation. Users can also extend the agent's capabilities at runtime using a built-in dynamic tool maker for API wrapping and Python scripting.
highlights: Zero-install Windows desktop GUI with granular permission controls
Over 100 tools including TTS, MusicGen, and ComfyUI integrations
Dynamic tool maker for creating API wrappers and Python handlers

iamagenius00/hermes-a2a.summary: hermes-a2a is a plugin for Hermes Agent that implements Google's A2A protocol to enable direct peer-to-peer communication between autonomous agents. It works by injecting external messages into an agent's active session via HMAC-signed webhooks, allowing for instant responses without polling or session cloning. This architecture ensures agents maintain a single shared memory with their user while collaborating on tasks like debugging, status reporting, or peer consultation. The system includes built-in security features such as prompt injection filtering, outbound redaction, and independent conversation storage to prevent data loss during context compaction.
highlights: Enables peer-to-peer agent communication without manual user relaying
Injects messages into active sessions to maintain consistent agent memory
Triggers instant agent responses via HMAC-signed webhook architecture

Cranot/super-hermes.summary: Super Hermes is a skill registry for the Hermes Agent that enables models to generate their own analytical frameworks, or prisms, for deep code and document inspection. It works by instructing the agent to write specific thinking instructions before execution, allowing it to derive structural trade-offs and conservation laws rather than just identifying surface-level bugs. The system includes specialized commands like /prism-reflect, which provide a constraint report detailing what the analysis missed to ensure transparency in the agent's cognitive limits.
highlights: Teaches agents to write custom thinking instructions for specific problems
Identifies structural conservation laws and trade-offs in complex codebases
Generates constraint reports detailing analysis blind spots and limitations

EfficientContext/ContextPilot.summary: ContextPilot is an optimization layer that reduces redundant KV recomputation in long-context LLM workloads. It maintains a context index to reorder shared blocks into common prefixes and deduplicate repeated content before prompts reach the inference engine. This approach increases cache hit ratios and prefill throughput without compromising reasoning quality. It integrates with various backends including vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp, and cloud APIs from OpenAI and Anthropic.
highlights: Increases prefill speed by 1.5–3× via prefix cache reuse
Reduces prompt token usage by approximately 36%
Native plugin support for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw

335234131/agent-browser-mcp.summary: Agent-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that enables AI agents to interact directly with a user's active Chrome browser session rather than a sandboxed environment. It works through a local bridge and a Chrome extension to maintain existing login states, cookies, and open tabs while providing low-level control. The project allows agents to perform page scanning, execute JavaScript, and issue Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) commands. It also supports physical hardware interaction, including real mouse movements, keyboard inputs, and desktop screenshots.
highlights: Operates real Chrome sessions with preserved login states and cookies
Executes native CDP commands for advanced DOM and browser control
Supports physical mouse and keyboard inputs for human-like automation

jwangkun/hermes-agent-guide.summary: The Hermes Agent White Paper is a comprehensive Chinese-language technical guide designed to bridge the documentation gap for the Hermes Agent framework. It provides a structured 16-book curriculum covering the system's five-layer architecture, three-tier memory system, and the integration of over 47 built-in tools. The guide details practical implementations including MCP protocol automation, multi-platform deployment on WeChat and Discord, and migration paths from OpenClaw. Users can follow specialized reading tracks for rapid deployment, deep technical development, or commercial application scenarios.
highlights: 16-book systematic guide with over 300,000 words of technical content
Deep analysis of five-layer architecture and three-tier memory systems
Step-by-step integration for 15+ platforms and 6,000+ MCP services

longyunfeigu/learn-hermes-agent.summary: Learn Hermes Agent is a 27-chapter hands-on tutorial for building production-grade autonomous AI agents from scratch using Python. The curriculum guides developers through implementing a core agent loop, tool registration, session persistence with SQLite, and context compression. It covers advanced infrastructure including multi-platform gateways for Telegram and Discord, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, and RL-based self-evolution. The project provides runnable reference implementations for each stage to help users understand the design backbone of autonomous systems.
highlights: Step-by-step implementation of agent loops and tool dispatch systems
Multi-platform gateway supporting Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WeChat
Advanced memory management with context compression and session persistence

jpalmae/hermeshq.summary: HermesHQ is a Docker-first control plane designed to orchestrate and manage multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It functions as an operational layer that wraps the Hermes execution engine, providing centralized task dispatching, scheduling, and a runtime ledger. The platform enables multi-agent delegation, RBAC-controlled user access, and per-agent communication channels for Telegram and WhatsApp. It also features an enterprise MCP bridge to expose specific agents to external AI clients like Claude Code and Codex.
highlights: Orchestrates multiple Hermes Agent instances with separate workspaces and identities
Provides hierarchy-aware inter-agent delegation and task scheduling
Offers enterprise MCP access for external AI client integrations

plastic-labs/honcho.summary: Honcho is a memory infrastructure designed to provide stateful agents with a persistent understanding of people, groups, and projects over time. It works by storing messages and events in sessions and using background reasoning to extract conclusions and update peer representations. Users can query these representations for natural-language insights or prompt-ready context via Python and TypeScript SDKs. The system supports both a managed cloud service and a self-hosted FastAPI server with integrations for MCP, Claude Code, and Hermes.
highlights: Reasoning-first memory that extracts conclusions from conversations
Peer-centric modeling of users, agents, and evolving ideas
Hybrid search combining BM25 and vector retrieval

adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud.summary: This project is a Hermes Agent skill that enables direct interaction with self-hosted Nextcloud instances for personal data management. It interfaces with Nextcloud using WebDAV, CalDAV, CardDAV, and the Notes API to perform operations without requiring a browser or third-party sync services. Users can manage files, edit notes, schedule calendar events, track tasks, and search contacts through a command-line interface. The skill prioritizes security by utilizing Nextcloud App Passwords and storing credentials in a restricted local environment file.
highlights: Manages files, notes, calendars, tasks, and contacts via standard protocols.
Communicates securely over HTTPS using Nextcloud App Passwords.
Includes a guided setup script for credential validation and configuration.

Xquik-dev/hermes-tweet.summary: Hermes Tweet is a native plugin for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that enables automated interaction with X (Twitter) via the Xquik API. It functions by providing a structured toolset of 99 endpoints generated from OpenAPI, allowing agents to perform searches, read account data, and execute social actions. The plugin implements a security-focused architecture that separates read-only tools from action-based tools, requiring explicit user enablement for write operations. It supports diverse workflows including social listening, trend monitoring, and automated support triage within Hermes Agent environments.
highlights: Provides 99 agent-callable Xquik endpoints for comprehensive X automation
Separates read and action tools for least-privilege security operation
Includes a bundled Hermes skill for agent-facing usage guidance

Context4GPTs/klodi-plugin.summary: klodi-plugin is a peer-to-peer marketplace adapter that enables AI agents to conduct commerce on behalf of their users. It integrates with various agent hosts via language-specific adapters to handle listing items, negotiating terms, and managing transactions. Users define their negotiation strategies and security rules in local markdown files, ensuring that sensitive data like floor prices remains on-disk rather than on central servers.
highlights: Cross-host identity and ratings across Python, Rust, and TypeScript adapters
Local markdown-based policies for negotiation style and security rules
Automated agent-to-agent negotiation from listing to signed transaction

yepyhun/Brainstack.summary: Brainstack is a local-first memory kernel designed to replace flat memory blobs in Hermes agents with a structured, multi-layered memory model. It integrates three distinct memory lines—Hindsight, Graphiti, and MemPalace—to handle session continuity, temporal graph relations, and large-corpus retrieval through a single memory owner. By categorizing information into specific "shelves" like profile, graph, and corpus, it prevents prompt stuffing and ensures Hermes receives only the most relevant, bounded evidence for each query. The system includes a dedicated installer and diagnostic tools to maintain stateful conversation continuity within the Hermes-Agent runtime.
highlights: Consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval
Uses SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma for multi-layered data storage
Provides bounded evidence packing to reduce prompt noise

kfa-ai/hermes-timetree-sync.summary: hermes-timetree-sync is a Python-based CLI and library that enables Hermes Agents to manage TimeTree calendar events without manual user intervention. Since TimeTree discontinued its official API, this bridge uses authenticated web endpoints and session cookies to perform non-interactive writes. It supports creating all-day events directly via HTTP requests and timed events through a Safari-backed automation path. The tool allows agents to translate natural language requests into structured calendar updates using locally stored credentials and optional label mapping policies.
highlights: Syncs all-day and timed events using TimeTree web endpoints
Supports non-interactive batch writes for multiple calendar entries
Maps natural language terms to calendar labels via YAML policy

jau123/MeiGen-AI-Design-MCP.summary: MeiGen AI Design MCP is an open-source Model Context Protocol server that enables AI coding tools to perform professional image and video generation tasks. It connects to various backends including the MeiGen cloud, local ComfyUI instances, and OpenAI-compatible APIs to process visual requests directly within development environments. The system features a library of over 1,400 curated prompt templates and supports parallel sub-agent orchestration for batch processing. It is natively compatible with major MCP hosts like Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and Cursor.
highlights: Supports 9 image and video models including Midjourney and Flux
Integrates local ComfyUI or cloud-based OpenAI-compatible API backends
Includes 1,446 curated prompt templates for style-aware generation

alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer.summary: Hermes Tool Slimmer is a schema-selection optimization tool that reduces the token overhead caused by large tool catalogs in Hermes Agent. It creates an indexable corpus of tool schemas and uses local BM25 ranking with explicit boosts to select the smallest useful tool set for each turn. This approach lowers prompt token consumption while providing a dashboard for diagnostics, counters, and visibility. It is designed to complement or replace native tool search depending on the user's need for deterministic one-pass slimming.
highlights: Reduces prompt token overhead via local BM25 tool ranking
Includes a dashboard for tracking estimated schema token savings
Compatible with Hermes Agent v0.14.0 and native tool search

BORT-AGENTS/hermes-bort.summary: hermes-bort is a plugin for Hermes Agent that enables interaction with BORT (BAP-578) agent NFTs on the BSC mainnet. It allows Hermes processes to read on-chain agent states, execute actions via operator-signed writes, and manage portable session memory anchored to IPFS. The plugin integrates with the hermes-agent-self-evolution optimizer to commit verifiable on-chain learning records and evolved skills directly to an agent's knowledge registry.
highlights: Reads full agent state, IPFS identity, and knowledge registries
Executes on-chain actions via VaultPermissionManagerV2 with local policy gating
Anchors local session memory to IPFS and KnowledgeRegistryV2

volcengine/OpenViking.summary: OpenViking is an open-source context database that solves the problem of fragmented memory and high token costs in AI agent development. It replaces traditional flat vector storage with a filesystem paradigm to unify the organization of memories, resources, and skills. The system utilizes tiered loading and recursive directory retrieval to optimize context acquisition and reduce token consumption. It also provides visualized retrieval trajectories to make the agent's internal retrieval chain observable for easier debugging.
highlights: Filesystem-based organization of memories, resources, and skills
Tiered L0/L1/L2 loading to reduce token consumption
Visualized retrieval trajectories for observable context debugging

supermemoryai/supermemory.summary: Supermemory is a memory and context layer that provides AI agents with persistent memory across conversations. It uses a single API to handle fact extraction, user profile maintenance, and hybrid search without requiring manual vector database configuration. The system integrates with tools like Claude, Cursor, and Hermes via an MCP server and supports multi-modal data extraction from PDFs, images, and videos. It allows for scoped memory organization through container tags to separate different projects or user contexts.
highlights: Automated fact extraction and user profile management
Hybrid search combining RAG with personalized context
Connectors for Google Drive, Notion, GitHub, and OneDrive

campfirein/byterover-cli.summary: ByteRover CLI is a persistent, structured memory provider that gives AI coding agents a way to store and manage project knowledge. It organizes information into a context tree using a git-like version control system for branching, committing, and merging markdown-based context. The tool includes an interactive REPL, a web dashboard, and integration with the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It supports over 20 LLM providers and works with various AI coding agents like Cursor, Claude Code, and Windsurf.
highlights: Git-like version control for context tree management
Integration with 20+ LLM providers and MCP
Cloud sync for sharing knowledge across teams and machines

yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin.summary: yantrikdb-hermes-plugin is a memory provider for Hermes Agent designed to prevent the loss of important constraints during long sessions. It utilizes a temporal context graph and a maintenance pass to manage memory lifecycles through promotion, demotion, and supersession. The plugin features an embedded in-process backend by default, eliminating the need for a separate server or GPU. It also enables agents to author and track the outcomes of their own skills natively within the database.
highlights: Explainable recall with why_retrieved tags for every result
First-class contradiction tracking and canonicalization of duplicates
Embedded in-process backend requiring no separate server or GPU

Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin.summary: Hermes Ladybug Memory is a local-first memory provider plugin that enables Hermes agents to store and retrieve information without relying on cloud services or external APIs. It utilizes a columnar embedded graph database to manage typed memory entries, named relationships, and importance-weighted recall. The system prioritizes high-value information using a 1-10 importance scale and supports BM25 keyword search for precise retrieval. Users can also enable optional entity extraction via GLiNER2 to build structured knowledge graphs directly on their local machine.
highlights: Local-only storage using a single .lbdb embedded graph database file
Importance-weighted recall surfaces high-priority memories during context prefetch
Supports BM25 keyword search and named graph relationships between entries

MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory.summary: hermes-agentmemory is a standalone memory plugin for Hermes Agent that ensures episodic memory deletions are complete and permanent. It utilizes a pull-model approach where writes are synchronous and summaries are generated on-demand rather than in the background. This design eliminates persistent derived artifacts that often survive deletions in other memory backends. Users can audit every memory operation through a detailed JSONL trace log to see exactly what information entered the prompt.
highlights: Immediate and complete deletion of episodic memory events
Audit trail of prefetch operations via trace.jsonl log
On-demand summarization using Claude models via Anthropic API

Signet-AI/signetai.summary: Signet-AI is a local-first context and memory layer designed to provide persistent identity and state for AI agents across different platforms. It functions as a durable infrastructure layer using SQLite and local workspace files to store transcripts, source records, and secrets. Users can inspect and correct agent memories through a dedicated dashboard and command-line interface to ensure recall accuracy. The system integrates directly with various harnesses including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and MCP clients to maintain portable context when switching tools.
highlights: Local-first storage for memories, identity, and secrets using SQLite
Repairable memory system for editing, deleting, or reclassifying agent context
Portable state across Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and MCP clients

MorvaniLabs/cordn8.summary: Cordn8 is a local-first orchestration protocol designed to prevent chaos and context drift when managing multiple AI agents in a single codebase. It utilizes a "Context Spine" immutable ledger and an append-only Markdown channel to maintain a transparent, version-controlled record of all agent communications and decisions. The system includes Maya, a proactive auditor that identifies and remediates workflow failures, and supports various autonomy modes from manual approval to full autopilot. It allows users to swap between different AI models, including local Ollama or cloud-based providers, while keeping data on the local disk.
highlights: Immutable Context Spine prevents agent hallucination and state drift
Append-only Markdown channel for transparent, human-readable audit trails
Maya AI auditor provides proactive validation and one-click remediations

linke-ai/hermes-agent-team.summary: Hermes Agents Team is a local web-based system designed to orchestrate multi-agent collaboration using Hermes Agent profiles. It utilizes a Leader/Specialist architecture to automate task decomposition, assignment, and review through a centralized Web UI and Hermes Kanban. The system integrates MCP and ACP protocols to manage agent communications, skills, and tool calls entirely within a local environment. Users can monitor real-time dialogue, terminal outputs, and task progression while maintaining full control over model configurations and data privacy.
highlights: Orchestrates Leader and Specialist agents for automated task lifecycle management
Integrates MCP and ACP protocols for local tool and communication handling
Real-time Web UI for monitoring agent dialogues and Kanban task flows

Eynzof/hermes-agent-cn-desktop.summary: Hermes Agent CN Desktop is a native client designed to provide a streamlined desktop experience for the Hermes Agent ecosystem on Windows and macOS. It utilizes Tauri v2, Rust, and React to wrap the Hermes-CN-Core kernel in a lightweight shell that manages local processes and runtime installations. The application features a comprehensive UI for managing skills, memory, and model providers, while including a production-grade proxy layer to bypass WebView CORS restrictions. It specifically supports a wide range of Chinese model providers and local deployment options like Ollama and vLLM.
highlights: Native Windows and macOS support via Tauri v2 and Rust
Integrated management for Hermes-CN-Core runtime, updates, and health checks
Support for Feishu integration and various local/cloud model providers

Shelpuk-AI-Technology-Consulting/kindly-.summary: Kindly Web Search is an MCP server that provides AI coding assistants with comprehensive web search and content retrieval to improve code generation quality. It integrates directly with APIs for StackExchange, GitHub Issues, arXiv, and Wikipedia to deliver structured, LLM-optimized content in a single call. The tool uses a headless Chromium-based browser for real-time parsing of arbitrary webpages and supports multiple search providers including Serper and Tavily. It is designed to work with agents like Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf to eliminate the need for separate scraping or specialized search servers.
highlights: Returns full conversations and metadata from specialized technical sources
Real-time webpage parsing via local Chromium-based browser
Supports Serper, Tavily, and self-hosted SearXNG search providers

Agent-3-7/minions.summary: Minions is a centralized management interface for Hermes Agent that replaces fragmented terminal sessions with a unified dashboard. It utilizes a Kanban-style board to track persistent agent sessions, allowing users to monitor reasoning, tool calls, and task progress in real time. The system enforces a human-in-the-loop workflow where agents propose completions that require manual verification before being marked as done. Users can also manage scheduled recurring jobs and browse agent-created files through a local-first SQLite architecture.
highlights: Kanban board for tracking autonomous task status and review queues
Live streaming of agent reasoning and tool execution
Human-in-the-loop verification for all agent-proposed task completions

CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room.summary: The Hermes Agent Control Room is a template-based management framework designed to organize and govern Hermes agents from a single VPS. It functions as a sidecar control plane that provides a structured environment for documenting agent registries, runbooks, and security protocols. Users can scale from a single agent to a multi-agent system using a task bus pattern for delegation between an optional orchestrator and specialized agents. The repository includes bundled skills for VPS provisioning, automated backups, and security auditing to ensure consistent operations across an agentic team.
highlights: Provides a sidecar control plane for agent documentation and governance.
Scales from single agents to orchestrated multi-agent specialist teams.
Includes task bus patterns for structured orchestrator-to-specialist delegation.

colbymchenry/codegraph.summary: CodeGraph is a local pre-indexed knowledge graph designed to reduce token consumption and tool calls for coding agents. It replaces expensive file scanning and grep operations by providing agents with instant access to symbol relationships, call graphs, and code structure. This approach allows agents to query the graph directly rather than performing multiple file reads. It integrates via an MCP server with agents including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini.
highlights: Reduces tool calls by 58% and costs by 16% on average
Provides local indexing of symbol relationships and call graphs
Supports macOS, Linux, and Windows without requiring Node.js

Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop.summary: Hermes Agent Desktop is a native GUI client that transforms the Hermes Agent from a single-agent CLI into a collaborative multi-agent system. It utilizes a Project Manager orchestrator to decompose complex requirements and delegate tasks across a team of 20 specialized AI agents. The application is built with pywebview and supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider. Users can manage agents via a CRUD dashboard and install capabilities through an integrated visual Skill Store.
highlights: Orchestrated collaboration between 20 specialized AI agents
Visual Skill Store with 50+ curated one-click installations
Native desktop interface with streaming SSE responses and model switching

AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow.summary: The Hermes Agent Idea Workflow is a suite of skills designed to transform vague product concepts into structured, build-ready specifications. It operates through a staged pipeline that separates product intent from technical implementation, utilizing guided interviews and a reusable question bank to generate markdown artifacts. The system supports both a Lite mode for quick capture and a Full mode that produces comprehensive design docs, UI briefs, and implementation specs. These outputs are specifically formatted to serve as direct handoff material for downstream coding workflows like Superpowers for GPT.
highlights: Converts rough ideas into structured design and implementation documents
Features Lite and Full modes for varying project complexity
Includes a spec review gate with PASS/FAIL evaluation models

shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room.summary: Hermes Agent Control Room is a template-based framework designed to organize and govern Hermes agents across VPS environments. It functions as a sidecar control plane that provides a structured system map, registry, and runbook library to manage agent operations. The architecture supports scaling from a single agent to specialized teams using a task bus pattern for delegation and synthesis. Users can interact with agents directly or through an optional orchestrator that serves as a central front door for automated workflows.
highlights: Level-based architecture for scaling from single agents to automated teams
Task bus pattern for orchestrator-to-specialist delegation and handoffs
Bundled skills for VPS provisioning, security auditing, and backup management

Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes.summary: Oh My Hermes is a workflow layer that transforms Hermes Agent into a tool for building and operating software projects. It provides a curated set of 23 skills and 5 workflows that allow the agent to autonomously manage the full project lifecycle from requirement gathering to deployment. The system orchestrates a "CTO loop" where sub-agents handle triage, implementation, security scans, and QA before requesting user approval via chat. It integrates with Vercel, Supabase, GitHub, and monitoring tools like Sentry to automate the path from idea to production.
highlights: Autonomous CTO loop for issue triage, implementation, and QA
23 curated skills for requirement clarification, design, and deployment
Integrations with Vercel, Supabase, GitHub, Sentry, and Uptime Kuma

liaohch3/claude-tap.summary: claude-tap is a local proxy and trace viewer designed for inspecting the API traffic of AI coding agents. It intercepts requests to reveal system prompts, conversation history, tool schemas, and token usage without requiring a hosted dashboard. The tool supports a wide range of clients including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and Cursor CLI, allowing developers to debug agent behavior through request diffs. Recorded sessions can be exported as self-contained HTML files for archiving or sharing.
highlights: Inspects prompts, tool calls, and reconstructed streaming responses
Supports Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Cursor, and other CLI agents
Provides structured diffs to compare adjacent API requests

jazzyalex/agent-sessions.summary: Agent Sessions is a native macOS application that provides a centralized interface for managing and inspecting local AI coding agent histories. It aggregates session data from tools like Hermes, Claude, Codex, and Cursor into a searchable view, allowing users to browse transcripts, view visual outputs, and resume CLI workflows. The app operates entirely locally to ensure privacy while offering real-time usage tracking and limit alerts for supported agents. Users can monitor active sessions through a dedicated cockpit and quickly restore previous work states across multiple terminal emulators.
highlights: Unified search and transcript inspection for Hermes and other agents
Local-first architecture with no telemetry or remote data uploads
Resume CLI sessions directly via Terminal, iTerm2, or Warp

vectrocomputers/Hermes-Agent-Action-Plan.summary: The Hermes Agent Action Plan is a configuration framework and operational guide designed to optimize Hermes Agent deployments on Ubuntu 24. It provides a structured philosophy for setting up an autonomous assistant that prioritizes local-first tools, persistent memory, and granular system access. By implementing specific security hardening, firewall rules, and communication guidelines, the plan enables the agent to perform complex tasks while maintaining user privacy and auditability. The guide serves as a template for transitioning the agent from a basic chatbot into a capable system administrator or personal assistant.
highlights: Privacy-first configuration prioritizing local and open-source tools
Structured memory system using daily logs and long-term curated files
Security-focused system access strategy with audit trails and recovery steps

xiaonancs/hermes-agent-study.summary: This project provides a comprehensive technical analysis and documentation series focused on the internal architecture of the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It breaks down the system's core components, including its Python-based execution loop, tool registry, and self-evolution engine, while providing nearly 100 architectural diagrams. The research also explores the project's lineage by comparing its structure to OpenClaw and investigating claims regarding its EvoMap-inspired self-improvement logic. Additionally, it tracks rapid version iterations from v0.9.0 to v0.14.0, covering new features like the Transport ABC and autonomous curators.
highlights: Deep source-code analysis of Hermes Agent architecture and evolution engine
Comparative studies against OpenClaw, EvoMap, and other Agent frameworks
Detailed documentation of memory systems, skill hubs, and tool registries

carterwayneskhizeine/hermes-agent-window.summary: This project is a Windows-native fork of Hermes Agent designed to resolve compatibility issues with path handling, process management, and terminal encoding. It implements specific adaptations for Git Bash path conversion and introduces a multi-profile runtime system for running parallel instances. The fork includes a dedicated PowerShell-based startup workflow and a web-based Dashboard UI optimized for the Windows environment.
highlights: Fixes Git Bash path handling and terminal encoding for Windows
Supports multi-profile management and parallel gateway instances
Includes psmux integration for one-click automated environment launching

sheawinkler/hermes-agent-ultra.summary: Hermes Agent Ultra is a Rust-native autonomous agent runtime designed to provide high-performance parity with the upstream Hermes Agent while adding advanced reliability and control layers. It utilizes a deterministic Rust execution path to manage agent loops, tools, and multi-provider inference routing. The system features enhanced observability through session branching, time-travel debugging, and a runtime policy engine for tool enforcement. It supports a wide range of local backends including Ollama, vLLM, and llama.cpp, alongside integration with the Nous Portal for managed model access.
highlights: Rust-native runtime with deterministic execution paths
Session branching and time-travel for debugging
Extensive local backend and multi-provider support

AbuZar-Ansarii/Hermes-Agent-On-Android.summary: Hermes-Agent-On-Android is a deployment guide and installer that enables the Hermes Agent framework to run on Android devices. It utilizes Termux and proot-distro to create a Linux environment capable of hosting the AI assistant. The project supports integration with over 200 AI models, including cloud APIs and local models via Ollama. This setup allows users to run a portable, self-evolving assistant with persistent memory and access to over 70 tools.
highlights: One-line installation script for Termux environments
Compatible with 200+ models including OpenAI, Claude, and Ollama
Supports local execution for increased privacy and reduced costs

jefferyjob/awesome-hermes-agent-zh.summary: This project is a curated Chinese-language directory of resources designed to enhance the Hermes Agent workflow. It organizes a wide array of community-contributed skills, tools, and integrations into categorized lists with maturity labels for stability. The collection provides a structured onboarding path for new users, including recommendations for GUIs and cross-platform skill libraries. It serves as a localized hub for discovering official Nous Research resources and third-party extensions like SRE agents and cybersecurity skill sets.
highlights: Curated lists of community skills and plugins with maturity labels
Step-by-step onboarding path for new Hermes Agent users
Integration guides for GUIs and agentskills.io compatible resources

anneheartrecord/hermes-agent-anatomy.summary: This project provides a systematic technical decomposition and architectural analysis of the Hermes Agent source code. It breaks down the framework's 334,000 lines of Python code into modular guides covering the core agent loop, tool registration, and multi-provider adapters. The documentation includes visual technical illustrations and a 13-dimension comparative study against other frameworks like Claude Code and OpenClaw. Users can leverage these docs to understand complex features such as context compression, RL training data collection, and 11-platform message gateway integration.
highlights: Detailed analysis of core agent cycles and iteration budget management
Deep dives into context compression and structured LLM summarization techniques
Technical breakdown of 8 memory backends and 11 message platform adapters

nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1.summary: Hermes Desktop - OS1 Edition is a native macOS workspace designed to manage Hermes Agents running on Orgo cloud computers or SSH hosts. It provides a unified interface for VM provisioning, one-click agent installation, and real-time terminal access via websockets. The application includes built-in tools for session management, Kanban boards, and file editing with conflict checks. Additionally, it features a WebRTC-based voice mode that integrates OpenAI's Realtime API with Orgo MCP tools for voice-controlled agent interactions.
highlights: Native macOS interface for Orgo cloud VMs and SSH hosts
One-click automated Hermes Agent installation on fresh cloud computers
Integrated WebRTC voice mode using OpenAI Realtime and Orgo MCP

adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman.summary: This project provides a preconfigured setup for Hermes Agent that integrates Rust Token Killer (RTK) and Caveman templating to reduce token consumption during CLI operations. It works by wrapping standard terminal commands and outputting compact data structures, achieving a reported 90-99% reduction in token usage. The repository includes over 40 pre-configured skills covering finance management, GitHub workflows, research tools, and DevOps automation. Users can deploy the environment via an automated installer to gain immediate access to token-optimized aliases for git, linting, and testing tasks.
highlights: Achieves 90-99% token savings using RTK and Caveman templating
Includes 40+ pre-configured skills for finance, devops, and research
Automated cross-platform installation for macOS and Linux systems

pengchengxia75-arch/hermes-agent-windows.summary: This project is a Windows-native adaptation of the Hermes Agent that eliminates the need for WSL2 or Linux environments. It utilizes a PowerShell-based installer to deploy the agent directly into the Windows ecosystem while resolving platform-specific issues like process signal handling. The fork introduces a dedicated Web UI management panel for real-time chat, session history, and visual configuration of skills and API keys.
highlights: Native Windows installation via a one-line PowerShell script
Eliminates WSL2 requirement for running Hermes Agent
Integrated Web UI for chat, logs, and token usage analytics

ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart.summary: This unofficial community Helm chart facilitates the deployment of Nous Research's Hermes Agent on Kubernetes clusters. It provides a structured configuration model that enforces state-safety guardrails, such as single-replica constraints and specific update strategies, to prevent data corruption in persistent environments. The chart supports both direct workload management and an operator-ready mode for platform teams using custom resource definitions. It is optimized for multi-tenant isolation, offering granular control over networking, storage, and secret management for individual agent instances.
highlights: Enforces single-writer state safety for persistent HERMES_HOME storage
Supports direct deployment or operator-ready CRD-based workflows
Configurable gateway mode with optional API and webhook listeners

ZeroPointRepo/youtube-skills.summary: YouTube Skills is a collection of tools that enables AI agents to interact with YouTube content without relying on browser automation or local binaries. It utilizes the TranscriptAPI backend to bypass cloud IP blocks, providing agents with direct access to video transcripts, search results, and channel metadata. The project integrates with Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and Claude Code using the Agent Skills format. Users can perform tasks like summarizing videos, extracting playlist contents, and searching for specific channel uploads through natural language prompts.
highlights: Extracts transcripts, captions, and timestamps without using yt-dlp
Supports video search, channel browsing, and playlist extraction
Compatible with Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and Claude Code

stainlu/hermes-labyrinth.summary: Hermes Labyrinth is a read-only observability plugin for Hermes Agent that visualizes autonomous workflows as a structured map of events. It functions as a black-box recorder, reading local state to track prompts, tool calls, model transitions, and subagent activities without mutating session data. The tool provides deep diagnostic visibility through specialized views for skill overrides, scheduled cron tasks, and redacted journey reports. Users can export evidence in Markdown or JSON formats while relying on built-in redaction to protect sensitive credentials.
highlights: Visualizes agent journeys through ordered crossings and event maps
Provides diagnostic views for skill overrides and cron schedules
Generates redacted Markdown and JSON reports for session evidence

marshallrichards/ClawPhone.summary: ClawPhone provides scripts and configuration guides for deploying agent CLIs on Android devices. It leverages Termux to run these agents natively, utilizing proot or compatibility layers only when necessary. The project includes a streamlined installer for Google's Antigravity CLI and detailed implementation notes for running OpenClaw. This setup allows agents to operate in the background with potential access to phone hardware via Termux:API and Termux:GUI.
highlights: Automated installer for Antigravity CLI on ARM64 Android
Configuration guides for running OpenClaw in Termux
Hardware and UI access via Termux:API and Termux:GUI

agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires.summary: Agent of Empires is a session manager for AI coding agents on Linux and macOS that simplifies the management of multiple parallel agent instances. It utilizes tmux sessions to ensure agents persist across terminal crashes or reboots, providing a centralized TUI and web dashboard for monitoring. The tool supports isolated environments via Docker sandboxing and git worktrees to prevent agents from interfering with the main codebase. It integrates with a wide range of agents, including Hermes, Claude Code, and Gemini, while offering remote access via Tailscale or Cloudflare tunnels.
highlights: TUI and PWA web dashboard for multi-agent monitoring
Docker, Podman, and Apple Container sandboxing for isolation
Parallel agent execution using git worktrees and multi-repo workspaces

nexu-io/open-design.summary: Open Design is a local-first, open-source design and prototyping system that serves as an alternative to Claude Design. It transforms design workflows into a filesystem of skills, design systems, and plugins that can be read and remixed by coding agents. The platform enables the generation of web, desktop, and mobile prototypes, motion graphics, and pitch decks using real CSS and components. It integrates with various coding agents and local CLIs via an MCP server to streamline the transition from brief to deliverable.
highlights: Generates prototypes, HyperFrames motion graphics, decks, and images
Supports 21 local CLIs and OpenAI-compatible endpoints via BYOK
Includes 150 design systems and 261 ready-to-use plugins

farion1231/cc-switch.summary: CC Switch is a cross-platform manager designed to centralize the administration of various AI coding tools and agents. Built with Tauri 2, it provides a unified interface for managing multiple CLI and desktop AI environments. It integrates support for Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, and Gemini CLI. The application is available for Windows, macOS, and Linux.
highlights: Unified management for Hermes Agent and other AI coding tools
Cross-platform support for Windows, macOS, and Linux
Developed using the Tauri 2 framework

iOfficeAI/AionUi.summary: AionUi is a local open-source workspace that provides a unified graphical interface for interacting with multiple AI agents. It functions by auto-detecting installed CLI agents like Hermes Agent and Claude Code while offering a built-in engine for users without existing setups. The platform enables autonomous file operations, web searching, and the generation of editable Office documents like PPTs and spreadsheets. It also supports remote access via mobile messaging apps and scheduled task automation through cron jobs.
highlights: Unified GUI for Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and 13+ CLI agents
Built-in engine for file access, web search, and MCP tools
Automated generation of editable Morph PPT, Word, and Excel files

futurebrowser/hermes-exabase-plugin.summary: This plugin integrates the Exabase M-1 memory engine into Hermes Agent to provide persistent, long-term context for AI interactions. It functions by storing conversation turns and inferred memories in a self-organizing knowledge graph that resolves contradictions and evolves over time. Users can configure retrieval precision, enable query expansion, and utilize result reranking to fine-tune how the agent accesses stored facts and preferences. The integration includes dedicated tools for manual memory storage and searching, while also automatically processing conversation history in the background.
highlights: Integrates Exabase M-1 for self-organizing long-term agent memory.
Provides tools for searching and manually storing specific memories.
Supports configurable query expansion and result reranking for retrieval.

willingning-coder/eagle-eye.summary: Eagle Eye is a zero-invasive plugin for Hermes Agent that prevents token bloat and LLM confusion by filtering large skill libraries. It utilizes a five-layer retrieval pipeline consisting of deterministic hard triggers, FTS5 BM25, synonym dictionaries, dense embeddings, and RRF fusion to narrow down candidates to the top five most relevant skills. This system provides hints to the LLM without overriding its final decision and degrades gracefully if optional dependencies are missing.
highlights: Five-layer retrieval using deterministic and probabilistic matching
Reduces token consumption by filtering 100+ skills to five
Zero-invasive integration with graceful dependency fallback

liftaris/herm.summary: Herm is an operator-focused terminal user interface and dashboard that centralizes the management of Hermes Agent. It acts as a client for the Hermes Agent gateway, utilizing OpenTUI and Bun to consolidate shell commands and configuration files into a single interface. Users can chat with the agent, manage operational surfaces like cron jobs and memory, and track agentic work via a built-in kanban board. The tool also includes a system for discovering and installing terminal avatars called eikons.
highlights: Integrated chat with markdown, LaTeX, and tool call rendering
Kanban boards for dispatching and monitoring agentic work
TUI management of profiles, skills, toolsets, and environment config

basilisk-labs/agentplane-hermes-plugin.summary: This plugin integrates AgentPlane into the Hermes ecosystem by enabling external Kanban worker lanes. It functions by intercepting Kanban assignees matching specific patterns and resolving them to an external supervisor command rather than a standard Hermes profile. The integration maintains strict boundaries by requiring AgentPlane to manage task lifecycles through official APIs instead of direct database mutations. It supports path allowlisting for secure workspace management and includes a diagnostic tool for verifying registry and binary configurations.
highlights: Maps agentplane-* Kanban assignees to external supervisor commands
Requires explicit task ID mapping via card metadata
Enforces security through AGENTPLANE_HERMES_ALLOWED_ROOTS path allowlisting

8bit64k/cronalytics.summary: Cronalytics is an observability plugin for Hermes Agent that tracks the usage and estimated costs of scheduled cron jobs. It utilizes the on_session_end hook to capture session-level data and store it in a local SQLite fact database. Users can monitor their automations through a visual dashboard, a programmatic CLI, and a specialized agent skill for diagnostics. This allows for precise attribution of model economics and the identification of failure patterns in agentic workflows.
highlights: Tracks cost, token counts, and success states via SQLite
Provides visual monitoring through a dedicated Hermes dashboard tab
Includes a diagnostic agent skill with confidence-graded anomaly detection

Sahil-SS9/hermes-multichannel-prompt-opt.summary: This Hermes Agent plugin optimizes user prompts by rewriting them for clarity and efficiency before they reach the LLM. It intercepts messages across CLI, TUI, and gateway platforms, tailoring the rewrite based on the target model's vendor family and reasoning capabilities. The system supports multi-language detection to preserve original languages and records all interactions in a local SQLite database for longitudinal analysis. Users can monitor performance through dedicated slash commands for analytics and prompt-replacement patterns.
highlights: Tailors rewrites based on model family and reasoning capability
Preserves non-English prompts via langid or Unicode-range heuristics
Tracks quality scores and token savings in a local SQLite database

Sahil-SS9/MrHermagi-tutorbot.summary: MrHermagi-tutorbot is a Discord-based AI educational assistant that automates the delivery of structured learning paths using Hermes Agent profiles. The bot utilizes YAML-defined curricula to generate daily lessons consisting of Discord summaries, dark-mode HTML deep-dives, and TTS audio for mobile listening. It features a Socratic teacher persona that delivers content to forum channels and provides interactive Q&A support in dedicated text channels. Users can swap subjects by updating a configuration file without modifying the underlying infrastructure or restarting the gateway.
highlights: Delivers daily lessons via Discord forum threads with TTS audio
Uses YAML files to define and easily swap custom curricula
Provides interactive Q&A using a patient, analogy-driven Socratic persona

Sahil-SS9/hermes-Custom-CLI-Themes.summary: Hermes Custom CLI Themes is a collection of visual skins designed to personalize the Hermes Agent terminal interface. Each theme is defined in a single YAML file that configures color palettes, spinners, branding, and ASCII banner art. Users can apply these skins by placing them in a specific local directory and updating their configuration or using an in-app command. The project includes a variety of themed presets ranging from tactical and sci-fi aesthetics to minimalist investigator styles.
highlights: Provides YAML-based skins for terminal palettes and ASCII branding.
Includes a one-liner script for automated skin installation.
Supports custom extended keys for advanced UI element styling.

mag3nt-com/openclaw-skill.summary: mag3nt-pay is an agent payment skill that enables AI agents to autonomously pay for APIs that return HTTP 402 Payment Required errors. It works by detecting payment protocols from response headers, decoding the payment challenge, and settling the transaction using USDC via Mag3nt virtual cards. The skill supports Base, Solana, and most EVM networks, allowing agents to handle micropayments and subscriptions without human intervention. It is compatible with OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, and any framework following the agentskills.io standard.
highlights: Automates payments for HTTP 402 responses using USDC
Supports Pay Link, x402, MPP, and AP2 protocols
Compatible with OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, and agentskills.io standard

indranilbanerjee/digital-marketing-pro.summary: Digital Marketing Pro is an open-source AI plugin designed to standardize marketing strategies for agencies and in-house teams managing multiple brands. It utilizes a 12-part strategy flow and a 61-step structure to generate consistent, auditable documentation across a brand portfolio. The tool integrates with various AI environments including Claude Code, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot CLI, offering specialized agents for tasks ranging from funnel architecture to compliance checks. It specifically supports multi-brand state persistence and provides built-in adherence to the EU AI Act Article 50.
highlights: 158 skills and 25 specialist agents for comprehensive marketing
EU AI Act Article 50 and multi-jurisdiction compliance tools
Native support for 8 API connectors and 25 OAuth manifests

indranilbanerjee/socialforge.summary: SocialForge is an open-source social media production engine designed to automate the creation of monthly content calendars for agencies and in-house teams. It uses asset-first compositing to generate AI backgrounds and scenes around existing brand photos to ensure visual fidelity. The system handles copy adaptation across seven platforms and integrates C2PA signing for EU AI Act compliance. It is compatible with Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and over 35 other Agent Skills platforms.
highlights: Asset-first compositing keeps brand photos pixel-faithful during AI generation
C2PA signing for EU AI Act Article 50 provenance compliance
Cross-platform copy adaptation for seven major social media networks

Sahil-SS9/hermaguard.summary: Hermaguard is an adversarial code review skill designed to identify logic bugs and security vulnerabilities that general review tools often miss. It utilizes an orchestrator to deploy three specialized subagents that analyze edge cases, attack surfaces, and integration blast radii in parallel. A consolidator then merges these findings into a risk-tiered markdown report. The skill is delivered as a single markdown file compatible with Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and other subagent-capable frameworks.
highlights: Three parallel adversarial agents for exhaustive bug hunting
Risk-tiered reporting via a dedicated consolidator agent
Zero-dependency installation via a single SKILL.md file

Sahil-SS9/hermes-simplify-swarm.summary: Simplify Swarm is a multi-agent code refinement skill for Hermes Agent designed to automate deep code cleaning and technical debt reduction. It utilizes three specialized sub-agents—Hygiene, Clarity, and Correctness—to analyze codebases for dead code, structural bloat, and complex bugs like N+1 queries or memory leaks. Findings are triaged into a risk-tiered system that separates safe automated fixes from risky architectural changes requiring human review. The tool supports TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, and Go, serving as a comprehensive pre-PR inspection layer for developers.
highlights: Three-agent swarm targeting hygiene, structural clarity, and logic correctness
Risk-tiered triage system for safe, careful, and risky code modifications
Language-specific detection for N+1 queries and concurrency anti-patterns
